answer:
\boxed{B}

The **"living instrument principle"** refers to the European Court of Human Rights' approach of interpreting the **European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR)** in light of contemporary conditions and evolving societal standards. **A v UK (2009)** is the landmark case where the Court applied this principle to **Article 8 (right to private life)** in the context of **gender recognition and trans rights**, expanding protections beyond traditional interpretations. This case exemplifies the Court’s dynamic adaptation of the Convention to modern challenges.
